---
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: letter
    geometry:
      - margin=0.5in
    fontsize: 9pt
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: "navy"
    urlcolor: "navy"
    include-in-header:
      - _header.tex
---

\thispagestyle{empty}

\BriefTitleBlock

\begin{multicols}{2}

\section*{Research Question}
Are blood concentrations of lead, cadmium, and mercury associated with HbA1c among U.S. adults aged 20+, and does dietary fiber intake modify these associations?

\section*{Data \& Sample}
\begin{tcolorbox}[callout=NHANES 2017–2018 (CDC / NCHS)]
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Population:} U.S. adults, age $\geq$ 20
  \item \textbf{Analytic sample:} N = 5{,}014
  \item \textbf{Exposures:} blood lead, cadmium, mercury
  \item \textbf{Outcome:} HbA1c (\%)
  \item \textbf{Effect modifier:} dietary fiber (g/day)
  \item \textbf{Survey design:} \texttt{SDMVPSU}, \texttt{SDMVSTRA}, \texttt{WTMEC2YR}
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Methods}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Direct CDC XPT download with retry; reproducible R pipeline
  \item Multi-file merge across 11 NHANES domains by \texttt{SEQN}
  \item Variable cleaning, exclusion tracking, QC checks
  \item Survey-weighted linear and logistic regression (\texttt{survey})
  \item Sequential adjustment: unadjusted $\to$ demographic $\to$ fully adjusted
  \item Lead $\times$ dietary fiber interaction analysis
  \item Publication-style tables and figures via Quarto
\end{itemize}

\section*{Key Result}
Fully adjusted models showed that the estimated lead–HbA1c association changed substantially after demographic, socioeconomic, behavioral, and nutritional adjustment, highlighting the importance of confounding structure in environmental epidemiology. The finding is reported as an adjusted statistical association, not evidence of a causal or protective effect.

\section*{Why It Matters}
This project demonstrates the workflow expected in an epidemiology data analyst role: cleaning and harmonizing public health data, documenting analytic decisions, implementing reproducible statistical models, producing publication-ready outputs, and interpreting findings cautiously.

\columnbreak

\section*{Sequential Model Adjustment}
\vspace{-4pt}
\begin{center}
\includegraphics[width=0.95\linewidth,keepaspectratio]{../../outputs/figures/fig04_forest_plot.png}
\end{center}
\vspace{-8pt}
\captionof{figure}{Estimated association between log blood lead and HbA1c across unadjusted, demographic-adjusted, and fully adjusted survey-weighted models. The direction reversal after adjustment underscores the role of confounding rather than a protective interpretation.}

\section*{Reproducibility}
\begin{tcolorbox}[callout=Engineering proof]
\begin{itemize}
  \item Public, version-controlled GitHub repository
  \item Full Quarto HTML \& PDF report in the repo
  \item GitHub Actions \textbf{Structure \& Setup Check}: passing
  \item GitHub Actions \textbf{Full Pipeline Render}: manual workflow
  \item Raw NHANES data excluded from Git; regenerated by script
  \item One-command reproduction: \texttt{Rscript run\_all.R}
\end{itemize}
\end{tcolorbox}

\section*{Skills Demonstrated}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Programmatic NHANES acquisition (\texttt{curl} + \texttt{haven})
  \item Complex survey analysis (\texttt{survey}, \texttt{srvyr})
  \item Multi-file harmonization on a single \texttt{SEQN} key
  \item Survey-weighted linear and logistic regression
  \item Effect-modification via continuous interaction
  \item Publication tables (\texttt{gtsummary}) and figures (\texttt{ggplot2})
  \item Reproducible Quarto report; GitHub Actions CI
\end{itemize}

\section*{Links}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Repo: \href{https://github.com/bobaoxu2001/nhanes-metal-nutrition-biomarkers}{github.com/bobaoxu2001/nhanes-metal-nutrition-biomarkers}
  \item Report: \href{https://github.com/bobaoxu2001/nhanes-metal-nutrition-biomarkers/blob/main/report/nhanes_metal_nutrition_biomarkers.html}{report/nhanes\_metal\_nutrition\_biomarkers.html}
  \item Summary: \href{https://github.com/bobaoxu2001/nhanes-metal-nutrition-biomarkers/blob/main/docs/executive_summary.md}{docs/executive\_summary.md}
\end{itemize}

\end{multicols}
